loc-mount-tremper.economic_state: austere; tribute flows in, little flows out
notable_features: a single heavy gate managed by the Shadow
current_events: recent visit from Grey Walker emissary
dangers: Preston's own presence (for those who displease him)
opportunities: audience with Preston (rare; granted for specific causes)
commissions from Whelan, Amar, or the Shadow
loc-mount-tremper.description: The site was a Cold War continuity-of-government bunker built into the mountain above the town. Preston seized it in the first month; his first act of governance was to reinforce its walls. A Species C artisan extended the audience hall with matter-manipulated stone that does not weather. The hall smells of cold air and machine oil. Preston rarely appears in it. Outside, the approach road is patrolled at three checkpoints. Visitors who pass the checkpoints are received at the outer barracks and processed through a series of formalities before the audience chamber. The formalities are not intimidation — they are calibration, and Whelan and Amar run them together.

loc-peekskill-reactor.economic_state: electricity production (partial grid ~10,000 households), fabrication exports

resources.description: only functional electrical grid in mid-Atlantic

current_events: reactor operational margins narrower than public
dangers: narrow reactor margins — unannounced failure risk [GM]
loc-peekskill-reactor.description: The complex was the Indian Point nuclear generating station. On Onset day its cooling systems failed. Kostas arrived on day 10 and stabilized it at the cost of his left face and arm. The reactor now operates as a peculiar hybrid — conventional fission cores with matter-manipulated containment and thermal management. The compound grew outward from Kostas's containment work. Walls laid with his hands in the first years still stand; his crews have added to them. The electrical grid reaches ten thousand households, a marvel in the region. What the households pay for the electricity is a matter of Bear-House policy that Kostas sets personally.

loc-yonkers-council-chamber.economic_state: modest surplus from biokinetic ag exports; healing-service revenue
notable_features: open-plan chamber (no walls between audience and administrative space)
Abel's sleeping bench in the corner of the chamber
Villanueva's private interview room (for foreseen injuries)
current_events: Three Judges' negotiation with Catskill Throne (informal)
opportunities: audiences with any of the Three Judges or Abel
loc-yonkers-council-chamber.description: The chamber is a pre-Onset community center converted to governance use. There are no gates or guards at the door. Citizens come with disputes and wait on benches. Morning is the busiest time; afternoons are reserved for complex cases. The Three Judges sit on three chairs that are not elevated above the chamber floor. Abel sleeps in the corner and rises when his attention is needed. The building is heated in winter by Obi's biokinetic work and ventilated in summer by the open windows. It smells of people and tea.

current_events: increased tribute to Catskill Throne this quarter
loc-kingston-hv.description: Kingston survived the collapse comparatively well — its population was already acclimated to semi-rural life, and the Hudson River provided transport continuity when roads failed. The Stockade District preserves enough pre-Onset structures to remind visitors of what towns looked like before. It is the primary Yonkers Compact trading town. Commerce flows daily. The Compact does not formally govern Kingston but its influence is pervasive and welcomed.

loc-rhinebeck.description: A small tributary town under Bear-House protection. Predominantly agricultural. [NEEDS AUTHORSHIP: specific character and current situation; referenced in bible but not detailed.]

notable_features: the falls (altered with faint water-affinity residue)
current_events: Marla seeks Bourse correspondence route bypassing Throne notice
dangers: proximity to Hollow Choir's range (eastern valleys)
loc-kaaterskill-falls.description: Marla's demesne is small by faction standards and conspicuous only for its location. The falls are one of the more striking natural features in the Catskills; Marla's water-affinity has subtly altered them. Her retinue is thirty armed personnel, mostly former neighbors. Catskill Throne collects token tribute and leaves her otherwise alone.

notable_features: valleys where Hollow Choir sub-audible vocalizations carry

connections.display_label: adjacent to Catskill Throne tributary polities

loc-hollow-choir-range.description: Three eastern Catskill valleys in which the Hollow Choir operates. Preston's patrols entered once, in month 8 of Year 1, and killed three cluster-bodies; the cluster reformed functional capability within weeks. Preston declared the valleys "ceded territory" and withdrew. They are now effectively lost to human access. Travelers who enter report a feeling of being listened to — the sub-audible vocalizations carry across the valleys. Most travelers who enter do not return.

loc-manhattan-midtown.controller: contested (Tower Lords via salvage crews; no governance)
notable_features: 42nd Street Line (informal border to Financial District)
Central Park Wildland (biokinetically altered; altered fauna; altered fish in lakes)
Metropolitan Museum of Art (partially looted, largely intact)
current_events: first rumors of movement in the Lower East Side ruins
loc-manhattan-midtown.description: Midtown is working ruin. The streets are clear enough for hand-carts; the larger avenues have been kept open by salvage crews for pull-cart routes. Most buildings are accessible. Some are structurally compromised. Crews working in paired buildings know each other by signals. The Metropolitan Museum stands mostly intact because its contents require specialist appraisal that no warlord employs. A few houses (Tanaka's particularly) have made quiet visits.

current_events: Echo has not moved in four years (stable)
rumors that deep-salvage runs have triggered events
loc-financial-district.description: South of Canal Street, memory begins to fail. The effect is progressive; a traveler who stays two hours will forget fragments. A traveler who stays eight hours will forget whole days. A traveler who stays a full night may not remember their own name. The vaults contain gold no one has retrieved. The archives contain records no one has read. The Echo does not manifest directly; its presence is inferred from its effects. No contact has been established. No communication has been attempted successfully.

current_events: Tower Lord interest in harvester licensing (unimplemented)
loc-central-park-wildland.description: The park has become a biokinetically altered ecosystem. Trees grow faster than baseline; undergrowth is thicker; fish in the lakes are larger and stranger. Harvesters work the park for altered material. Most return.

notable_features: heavy kinetic corruption in district cores
"street-reshaping" visible in parts of Crown Heights and East New York
loc-brooklyn-tower-districts.description: The districts read different depending on which Lord rules them. Crown Heights under Ronen Marcus has streets that no longer quite match their pre-Onset geography; the walls of buildings lean in directions that would require kinetic force-vector analysis to justify. Flatbush under Tenno is calmer but poorer. Bay Ridge under Elena is the best-maintained of the districts. Cross-district travel requires knowledge of which Lord holds which block and which greetings are acceptable at which checkpoints.

notable_features: the workshop is above the Flushing central market
loc-flushing-workshop.description: The workshop occupies the second floor of a narrow building above the Flushing market. A wrought-iron staircase leads up. Visitors are received in an anteroom with three chairs and a small table. The actual workshop is a long room with north-facing windows and benches for Kuan and his three primary apprentices. The anomalous object is not in the workshop. It is stored separately, in a location only Kuan and one apprentice know. Kuan studies it on specific days when he has told no one where he will be.

notable_features: Father Rhodes's parish in outer Queens
current_events: post-incident Voice network security audit ongoing
loc-queens-commonage.description: The Commonage is a federation of neighborhoods. Each neighborhood has its own local council; regional coordination happens through Voices (perceptual-primary individuals trained in broadband telepathy). The effect to an outside observer is that markets and community halls seem oddly synchronized — multiple merchants change prices at the same moment, multiple guards shift posts at the same moment. This is the Voice network at work.

notable_features: temporal-spatial logistics (goods appear without visible transport)
loc-staten-citadel.description: The harbor is quiet in a way visitors find unsettling. No bridge traffic. Goods arrive at the docks without visible transport — Chen's temporal-spatial crews drop crates into place. The harbor approach is defended by a combination of pre-Onset fortifications and newer matter-manipulated walls. The compound itself is adjacent to the Staten Island ferry terminal (converted to command use).

loc-jamaica-bay-fishery.description: A brackish estuary shared uneasily between the Queens Commonage and a minor Tower Lord. Both sides operate boats. Both sides avoid the other's boats when possible. Catch is good enough that neither will withdraw. [NEEDS AUTHORSHIP: specific landmarks and notable features.]

current_events: ongoing refusal of Tower Lord vassalage
loc-coney-island-holdout.description: A holdout community protected by a T6 biokinetic and a concentration of former lifeguards now serving as a militia. The Tower Lords have not yet moved against them — the cost/benefit has not justified it. The community runs small boats, grows vegetables in former parking lots, and maintains an explicit ethos of non-subordination.

loc-cross-queens-line.description: The western boundary of Queens Commonage territory and the eastern edge of Tower Lord reach. Roughly the Van Wyck corridor. Guarded by both sides. Smuggling is heavy. Violence is periodic.

loc-laguardia-ruin.description: A small cult-like community has made the ruin of LaGuardia airport its home. They believe civil aviation can be restored. They have been trying for four years and have produced no functional aircraft. The community is harmless and welcomes visitors who will carry news of pre-Onset aviation. It is a sad place.

notable_features: dread-field zone (extending ~30m from Volk at rest)
loc-port-newark-compound.description: The compound is a converted port warehouse complex. The main hall contains the iron circlet (Volk's crown, welded from harbor machinery). The dread-field is palpable within thirty meters of Volk. Visitors pass through it and feel their stomach drop; regulars have conditioned themselves against it with mixed success. The compound smells of salt and machine oil.

notable_features: Brick's compound at former ferry terminal
current_events: Brick's quiet outreach to Tower Lords
loc-jersey-city-waterfront.description: Brick Stepanski governs Jersey City as Volk's captain. Day-to-day administration is through his own men. The waterfront is active — salvage comes across from Manhattan, is staged for further transport. Brick's compound is at a former ferry terminal converted for his use.

loc-elizabeth-salvage-yards.description: Razorgrin runs Elizabeth with less overt violence than most Iron Crown captains. Her workshop produces sharpened metal goods prized regionally. The fact that she is Species H and serves Iron Crown is a topic she does not discuss.

loc-liberty-state-park-staging.description: The park has been converted to a staging area for Iron Crown's Manhattan salvage operations. Crews assemble here, ferry to Manhattan via paid Staten passage, and return with loads.

loc-old-market-philly.description: The Old Market occupies the converted 30th Street Station — pre-Onset train terminal, now trading hall. The upper gallery, where the Council of Thirty-One meets, was formerly a restaurant. The trading floor fills the main hall. Warehousing operations are adjacent in former baggage handling areas. In the morning the hall is noisy and in the evening it is quiet. The Bourse closes at dusk; the library stays open later for special scholars.

loc-bourse-library.economic_state: funded by Bourse levy; fee-for-service for external scholars
dangers: MINOR: loss of contact with GM-classified materials could trigger Fed Continuity action
loc-bourse-library.description: The library is above the Old Market in the former Post Office complex. It is quiet. Pilar Reyes runs it and rarely leaves. Scholars pay a fee for access; Bourse affiliates are free. The collection has grown substantially since Year 1 as salvage crews have recovered pre-Onset books from collapsed libraries.

connections.display_label: Delaware River; ocean via Chesapeake; Camden across river

loc-delaware-wharves.description: The Bourse's river operation runs from wharves south of the city center. Ships come in from Delmarva, Crabclaw, ocean trade; they leave with Bourse copper, salvage, coal. South Philly Holding patrols the wharves.

loc-philadelphia-navy-yard.description: Dreng's seat. The yard is partially operational — dry-docks are usable, some industrial facilities function. Dreng's retinue patrols. Anomalous objects occasionally surface from salvage; Dreng prefers to pass them up the chain to Bourse.

dangers: burn zones dangerous during containment operations
loc-fairmount-park.description: The park has been biokinetically transformed, with the upper reaches holding the Wissahickon Green Hive colony. Burn-crews work the perimeter year-round on Bourse contract, maintaining a buffer against the Hive's creep toward residential Philadelphia. Licensed harvesters work the altered flora zones for commercial materials.

loc-camden-nj.controller: mixed (House Pereira salvage-import; Species I Herrera-kin; informal Iron Crown pressure)
notable_features: The Broker eldritch entity in vicinity

connections.display_label: Philadelphia (Bourse bridge route); Pine Barrens (distant eldritch)

current_events: Doctor Pale's quiet operation; Iron Crown intimidation attempts (repulsed)
loc-camden-nj.description: Camden is divided. The riverfront is dominated by House Pereira's salvage-import operation; the interior has Herrera-kin neighborhoods; the outskirts are contested. Doctor Pale operates from a location nobody has reliably identified. The Broker has been observed at specific salvage sites offering pre-Onset mechanical objects in exchange for unspecified considerations.

notable_features: the library (largest functional collection in region)
loc-princeton-accord-hall.description: Nassau Hall has been repurposed as the Accord's civic center. The Council chamber is in the former chapel; library operations continue on the third floor. Dr. Okafor's offices are adjacent to the chamber. The campus generally remains a functional academic environment — scholars live there, students learn there, a modest printing press operates.

resources.description: Sparrowhand correspondence (T8 warped fox; research contact)

loc-rutgers-nb.description: The campus has been restructured around biokinetic agriculture training. Most pre-Onset buildings are repurposed; the athletic fields are now instruction plots. Students from across the region come to train. The ethnography department, still functional under Afolabi, collaborates formally with several Warped including Sparrowhand.

opportunities: research access with White Horse's permission
loc-princeton-horse-lands.description: Meadows and woodlands south of Princeton that the White Horse's herd occupies. The CJL treaty specifies that townships do not cultivate or hunt within these lands, and she acknowledges township jurisdiction elsewhere. Researchers from Princeton visit by appointment and carry a specific token Okafor issues.

loc-edison-township.description: Edison is the southern anchor of the Flushing-Edison Cluster and a full CJL member township. The Species C community coordinates through a sub-council under Twelve Hands authority. Most residents are baseline human; Species C presence is ~15% of population.

loc-toms-river.description: The southern anchor of the Central Jersey League. Proximate to the Pine Barrens perimeter; the CJL maintains active militia training here specifically against Listening expansion. The community is anxious but functional.

notable_features: Batsto Sprawl / Deep-Root (~50,000 tons Hive biomass)
current_events: cluster activity levels stable but monitoring is limited
loc-batsto-village.description: The pre-Onset historical reenactment village at Batsto was one of the sites where The Listening founded. The cluster and the sprawl now occupy it and its surroundings. Human entry is uniformly fatal unless the visitor is Listening-aligned.

notable_features: One Who Waits's ceremonial presence grounds
loc-wharton-forest-reserve.description: The core of The Listening. Cultists at various stages of transformation live here. One Who Waits moves among them periodically. The boundary with populated territory is not clear — cultists drift inward and outward.

loc-ongs-hat.description: The settlement was abandoned before the Onset. Pre-Onset it was the subject of conspiracy folklore (parallel-dimension experiments, etc.). Post-Onset, events there have been consistently anomalous. Visitors report impossible geometries, time dilation, and occasional entity contact. Bourse library occasionally commissions observations.

notable_features: visible change in forest character (blacker pines)

connections.display_label: CJL southern townships; Pine Barrens interior

current_events: line has crept outward maybe 500m in past year
loc-black-pines-line.description: The informal boundary between settled New Jersey and Listening territory. It is visible — past a certain line the pines are darker, the underbrush denser, the air quieter. Border settlements monitor the line and report creep.

resources.description: coal processing, agricultural market, Species G coordination

current_events: seeking Bourse burn-crew contract against Susquehanna Crown
loc-allentown.description: The largest Principality. The council chambers occupy a former municipal building. Veldt's offices are adjacent. Markets are active; the city has maintained pre-Onset street patterns and much of its residential character. The smell of coal smoke is noticeable in winter.

dangers: structural collapse; occasional altered fauna (Night-walker raccoons)
loc-bethlehem-steelworks-ruin.description: The old Bethlehem Steel site. Salvage operations have produced significant metal stocks over four years, but yields are declining as easy material is exhausted. The ruins are strangely picturesque — enormous rusted structures against the sky.

dangers: GM: pending discovery of Hive intrusion
loc-carbon-county-mine.description: An active coal mine. Biokinetic practitioners supplement ventilation and tunnel stability. Output is the Principalities' primary export. An undetected Hive root intrusion is present in a lower gallery [GM]; discovery may trigger a containment crisis.

loc-jim-thorpe.description: A defensible mountain Principality. Lord Meisner is the titular ruler but Halbrecht's coordination of the Species G community is the effective organizing principle for much of the town's internal life.

loc-susquehanna-crown-border.description: The active front against the Susquehanna Crown Hive. Militia patrols maintain a perimeter that moves ~1km inward every season. The Principalities are negotiating with Bourse for professional burn-crew contract.

connections.display_label: Andrews, Quantico, DC core; Species I Diaz-kin in Anacostia

loc-pentagon.description: The Pentagon is partially operational. The central plaza is now an outdoor meeting space; the rings are in various states of function. Alcott's command is in the inner rings. The fiction of a continuing federal government is maintained — date stamps, classification markings, signed orders.

resources.description: fuel storage (dwindling), munitions stockpile, hardened facilities

loc-andrews-afb.description: Andrews preserves the fiction of air command. Pilots meet, review non-existent schedules, maintain flight logs. The aircraft have not flown. A small cargo sub-command operates horse-cart logistics to the Pentagon.

loc-quantico-periphery.description: Quantico's periphery is Reeve's base of operations. His Irregulars drill there; stockpiles he draws on are adjacent. The fiction of subordination to Pentagon is maintained in paperwork but not in practice.

notable_features: active lending to scholars; Sternberg intelligence interest
loc-georgetown-library.description: Georgetown's library survived comparatively intact. Fed Continuity staffs it; Sternberg has an intelligence interest in what gets borrowed and by whom. Scholars correspond with Princeton and the Bourse.

loc-dc-mall.description: The Mall has been partially restored as a ceremonial civic space. Fed Continuity holds monthly observances. The monuments are mostly standing; the reflecting pool is dry. The space is a reminder of what the institution claims to represent.

loc-arlington-cemetery.description: Fed Continuity maintains Arlington as symbolic territory. The ceremonial implications are important to Alcott's institutional legitimacy. Maintenance staff tend the grounds.

resources.description: Species I healer network; Diaz-kin community

notable_features: Diaz-kin cultural center; Species I elder council
loc-anacostia.description: The neighborhood where Elder Diaz's kin is concentrated. The Species I community is the effective governing presence; Fed Continuity's formal authority is accepted but limited. Evacuation contingency plans exist against Reeve takeover.

loc-pg-county-md.description: PG County is the heartland of Species I Flores-kin and the largest coherent Species I concentration in the mid-Atlantic. Elder Flores's compound is a modest home in what was a middle-class suburb pre-Onset; it now serves as the informal center of kin governance.

loc-easton-md.description: Marsden's seat is a working farm that became the center of a demesne. Her manor is a renovated Victorian house. The altered cattle graze in pastures that visibly differ from pre-Onset pastures — richer, greener, subtly luminous in certain light.

loc-denton-md.description: Chen's seat is quiet. The seed bank occupies a reinforced facility at the edge of the town. Climate control is biokinetically maintained. Samples are tested in adjacent plots.

resources.description: agricultural production; Auratic calm-zone as de-facto mediation venue

notable_features: Archer's calm-field extends across much of demesne
current_events: ongoing mediation of Lord Finch–Marsden grain-pricing dispute
loc-chestertown-md.description: Archer's demesne feels different within minutes of entering. The calm is noticeable. Lord-level disputes are frequently brought to him for mediation, and he hosts them at his manor's main hall. His household is small, his retinue minimal. The calm does the work.

connections.display_label: Species I Diaspora; other Harvest Lords

loc-salisbury-md.description: Velasquez's seat blends a Harvest Lord demesne with a Species I community anchor. Salisbury is the Species I southern outpost; cultural events here are cross-kin. Velasquez is the only kin-member Harvest Lord and her seat reflects that duality.

dangers: Chesapeake Bog proximity; Salt Judge territorial restrictions
loc-rehoboth-beach-de.description: Finch runs a coastal demesne. The fishing fleet is the core of the economy; his beachfront hall is modest. The Salt Judge's restrictions constrain where boats can fish; the Chesapeake Bog Hive is a concern to the west.

connections.display_label: other Harvest Lords; regional Catholic communities

dangers: GM: if eldritch-adjacent children are discovered
loc-monastery-north-dover.description: Brother Tam's monastery is a working religious community that became a Harvest Lord demesne. Orphans are taken in; novices are trained. The contemplative-zone is subtle — visitors feel quieter. Three of the orphans are in partial eldritch transformation; Brother Tam has not told anyone outside the monastery.

resources.description: biokinetic-altered fishery; Baltimore harbor; pre-Onset industrial salvage

notable_features: promenade converted to trading quay; Inner Harbor lord's compound
loc-inner-harbor-baltimore.description: The Inner Harbor is the Crabclaw's commercial center. The pre-Onset promenade is a trading quay; biokinetic-altered catch offloads daily. The Inner Harbor lord's compound is a converted hotel tower on the harbor. Ships from Delmarva and Philadelphia are common.

notable_features: Dundalk lord's compound; active salvage operations
loc-dundalk.description: Dundalk specializes in industrial recovery and heavy-goods commerce. The Dundalk lord is less politically active than Inner Harbor but holds significant infrastructure.

loc-fort-mchenry-ruin.description: The pre-Onset Fort McHenry has been fortified as the Crabclaw's southern defensive line. It is also a neutral meeting ground for the three Crabclaw lords (one of the few locations they will gather).

resources.description: the riverboat clinic ("Miriam"); Rossi's medical capacity

current_events: possibly missing at game start (campaign seed
loc-delaware-river-mobile.description: Rossi's riverboat clinic travels the Delaware, stopping at small-town sites on a published schedule. Patients who cannot afford faction medical service bring what they can. The boat is modestly equipped; the medical care is competent. If the clinic is overdue, communities notice.

notable_features: pre-Onset highway infrastructure partly functional for carts
loc-cross-bronx-raider-territory.description: The Reaper's band operates from the Cross-Bronx corridor. Merchants avoiding the Cross-Bronx pay more for routes; merchants who use the Cross-Bronx hire escorts. Occasional large caravans repel the raiders, but most travelers are easier targets.

loc-trenton.description: Trenton is a CJL peripheral member. The Mapmaker operates from here; commissions are received through intermediaries. The pre-Onset capitol is partially functional as a township meeting space.

notable_features: Tomás Reyes's compound; Reyes-kin assembly hall

connections.display_label: Species I Diaspora; Delmarva (Lord Velasquez cross-tie)

loc-wilmington-de.description: Wilmington is Tomás Reyes's base. The Reyes-kin is smaller than Flores-kin but coherent. Reyes's compound is central; commerce and assembly occur in adjacent buildings. The town has a quiet energy of a place in political transition.

notable_features: location unknown; reached only through intermediaries
loc-catskill-reclusive.description: The Measurer's workshop is not publicly located. Clients reach him through a series of intermediaries. Work commissioned is project-specific and paid in copper or pre-Onset materials.

loc-outer-queens-parish.description: The parish occupies a repaired Catholic church in outer Queens. Father Rhodes's Auratic makes the hall feel safer than its construction warrants. Eighty orphans live in an adjacent school building. The community is mixed baseline and Species I with small Species H presence.

loc-philadelphia-area (Old Black's range.description: Old Black's range covers Philadelphia metro. He keeps preferred roosts near the Bourse hall where he can observe commerce and be easily reached by Bourse messengers. His corvid network extends outward into the Delaware Valley.

loc-lehigh-valley-interior (Old Badger's.description: Approximately 40 km² of forest and subterranean range in the Lehigh Valley interior. Treaty markers (stones) indicate the boundary. A specified meeting glade is where Veldt delivers the monthly pig; messages can be left there.

connections.display_label: Central Jersey League southern townships; Herrera-kin Camden periphery

loc-pine-barrens-perimeter (Seer Willem'.description: Seer Willem moves along the perimeter, taking shelter in a rotating set of discreet locations. Parties who want his information find him by leaving specific signs at known points.

notable_features: specific no-netting zones (unmarked but known); intermediary osprey
loc-chesapeake-bay-north (Salt Judge ran.description: The Salt Judge's enforcement is bay-scale but specific: fishing boats in certain zones are found sunk within days of violation. The zones are communicated to fleets by osprey messengers. Respectful fishing fleets have no problems.

loc-pine-barrens-mobile (Lost Thing rang.description: The Lost Thing's range is all of the Pine Barrens but unpredictable. It moves. It shifts in appearance. Encounters are rare and their outcomes are not predictable.

notable_features: specific meeting glades where Grey Walker can be approached

connections.display_label: Catskill Throne (two meetings); Yonkers; Species I

current_events: open to approach by serious listeners
loc-southern-catskills-wandering (Grey W.description: The southern Catskill wilderness where Grey Walker ranges. Meeting him requires going alone, unarmed, and willing to listen. He has specific glades where he will receive visitors. Finding them requires local knowledge or reliable referral.

current_events: ongoing observation of Helen; Fed Continuity contingency
loc-dc-potomac-district.description: A small village in the Potomac District where Little Helen and her family live. Irregulars observe from a discreet post. Fed Continuity observers watch from further out. The village is outwardly ordinary; the tension around the child is not.
